dashboard.title: Dashboard
agent.title: Agent Chat
agent.send_failed: Failed to send message. Please try again.
agent.empty_hint: Send your first message, or choose a mode for this conversation first.
agent.upload_attachment_failed: Failed to read the selected attachment.
agent.upload_attachment_image_requires_v: The current model does not support vision input, so image attachments cannot be sent as visual input.
agent.upload_attachment_word_legacy_unsu: Legacy Word `.doc` files are not yet supported for direct text extraction. Please use `.docx`.
agent.attachment_text_truncated_note: File contents were truncated to the inline limit.
agent.attachment_binary_note: This is a binary attachment. The current chat only includes metadata and does not inline the file contents.
agent.workspace_empty: This session workspace is still empty.
agent.workspace_scope_empty: This session workspace has not been prepared yet.
agent.workspace_scope_hint: This panel shows the real session workspace. The original project root remains available through the `project/` entry inside the workspace.
agent.workspace_no_preview: No inline preview is available for this file yet.
agent.workspace_load_failed: Failed to load workspace. Please try again.
agent.session_view_only_hint: Viewing a channel session; this view is for messages only
agent.sidebar_hint: Sessions are primary. Roles only define how a chat starts.
agent.roles_hint: Pick a mode before you start. It locks after the first message.
agent.sessions_hint: The main session is interactive. Channel sessions are view only.
agent.mode_toolbar_hint: Choose a mode before you start. It locks after the first message.
agent.execution_supervised_description: Allow execution, but riskier actions still need safeguards.
agent.execution_full_description: Let the agent execute autonomously for this chat session.
agent.approval_summary_pending: Agent wants to use {{tool}} and is waiting for your approval.
agent.approval_summary_approved_once: {{tool}} was approved for this step and will continue running.
agent.approval_summary_approved_always: {{tool}} was approved for future matching steps in this session.
agent.approval_summary_denied: {{tool}} was denied. Agent will try another path or stop this step.
agent.approval_summary_expired: The approval request for {{tool}} is no longer active.
agent.approval_hint: "Always allow" only applies to later matching steps in this chat session.
agent.approval_result_once: The one-time approval was sent. Agent will continue this step.
agent.approval_result_always: Future matching steps in this session will be allowed automatically.
agent.approval_result_denied: The denial was sent. Agent will try another path or stop this step.
agent.approval_result_expired: This request was not completed and is no longer active.
agent.role_banner_prefix: This chat will start in mode:
agent.roles_primary_only: Task modes only apply to the main chat. Channel sessions stay view only.
agent.role_switch_confirm: Switching modes will start a new main chat and clear the current main chat history. Continue?
agent.role_general_description: Best for everyday questions, planning, and general tasks.
agent.role_code_description: Focuses more on implementation, debugging, code analysis, and concrete steps.
agent.role_writing_description: Focuses more on synthesis, structure, clarity, and polished reusable output.
agent.role_picker_title: Choose a mode for this conversation
agent.role_picker_hint: The selected mode will shape the first turn of this new chat.
agent.role_picker_locked_hint: After the first message is sent, this conversation mode locks and can no longer be changed.
tools.title: System Capabilities
tools.name: Name
tools.page_hint: This page shows the agent's low-level system capabilities and the commands detected on this machine. It is primarily for diagnostics and advanced configuration, not a common task entry point.
tools.agent_tools_hint: These are the low-level capabilities the agent can call. Actual execution still depends on session permissions and security policy.
tools.cli_policy_hint: This list only shows CLI commands discovered on the system. Whether execution still needs approval mainly depends on supervised mode and command risk.

tools.skills.install_hint: Support git repository URL or local directory path.

skill_market.title: Unified Skill Market
skill_market.risk_desc: Skills from open marketplaces may contain insecure logic. Always audit and install only trusted sources.
skill_market.confirm_desc: You are installing {{name}} from an open marketplace source.
skill_market.acknowledge: I understand the risks and will audit this skill before production use.
skill_market.not_installable: This ClawHub skill currently supports browsing only in market list.
skill_market.items.open-skills-pack.name: Open Skills Pack
skill_market.items.open-skills-pack.desc: Community skills package for digital employee workflows.

config.title: Configuration
config.masked_desc: API keys, tokens, and passwords are hidden for security. To update a masked field, replace the entire masked value with your new value.
config.view.form_hint: Some advanced settings are only available in the TOML view.
config.form.provider_help_default: Choose Custom Provider only when you need to use your own endpoint.
config.form.provider_help_custom: Custom Provider is selected. Enter the endpoint below.
config.form.api_key_placeholder: Enter new API key to replace masked value
config.form.api_url_placeholder: Leave empty to use provider default
config.form.api_url_help_default: Most official providers can leave this empty unless you need to override the default endpoint.
config.form.gateway_port_help: This port must match the desktop app gateway connection.
config.form.gateway_host_help: Bind address for the gateway service.
config.form.gateway_require_pairing_help: Require clients to pair before accessing the gateway.
config.form.gateway_allow_public_bind_he: Allow binding the gateway to a public interface.
config.form.proxy_mode_help: Direct Connection: ZeroClaw does not use a proxy. Use System Proxy: ZeroClaw follows system proxy environment settings.
config.form.proxy_mode_migrated_hint: Legacy advanced proxy settings detected. Switching mode will overwrite them with the selected mode.
config.form.max_tool_iterations_help: Stops the agent after this many tool calls to avoid infinite loops.
config.form.max_history_messages_help: Limits how many messages are kept in each conversation.
config.form.model_context_window_tokens_: Manually set the context window size for the current default model. Leave empty or set 0 to remove the manual value and fall back to auto inference.
config.form.model_context_window_ttl_hel: How long cached model window metadata stays valid before refresh updates it.
config.form.tool_dispatcher_help: Controls how tools are encoded for the model.
config.form.parallel_tools_help: Run multiple tool calls in one iteration when supported.
deerclaw_settings.title: DeerClaw Settings
deerclaw_settings.description: Manage model access, agent behavior, and the global action budget in one place.
deerclaw_settings.agent_section_hint: This page holds the core runtime settings for the agent.
deerclaw_settings.max_actions_per_hour_h: Acts as a global fallback guardrail for total actions in one hour. Keep it high enough so normal tasks are not interrupted early.
config.form.autonomy_description: Global system settings now keep only stable guardrails. Session workspace choice and the supervised/full mode are configured per session, not here.
config.form.autonomy_session_note: Browser is built in and enabled by default. Workspace and path scope belong to the session flow; this page mainly defines global shell allow/block rules.
config.form.autonomy_level_help: Controls how proactively the main agent executes actions by default and when it should pause for approval.
config.form.autonomy_workspace_only_help: Restrict filesystem operations to the workspace.
config.form.autonomy_advanced_hint: Shell is built in. This page keeps only the command allowlist/blocklist so session permissions and system permissions stay separate.
config.form.autonomy_allowed_commands_he: One command name per line, e.g. git, cargo, cat. Both supervised and full still honor this list.
config.form.autonomy_blocked_commands_he: One command name per line, e.g. rm, sudo, curl. Matches here are never executed regardless of session mode.
config.form.autonomy_require_approval_he: Ask before medium-risk commands or actions run.
config.form.autonomy_block_high_risk_hel: Always block high-risk commands or actions.
config.form.browser_enabled_help: Allow the main agent to open a browser, access websites, and perform related actions.
config.form.browser_access_hint: Browser capability is part of the main agent's default execution scope. Limit which domains it may access and how it runs here.
config.form.browser_allowed_domains_help: One domain or subdomain per line. Leave empty to deny all.
config.form.browser_backend_help: For most users, choose Auto. computer_use is for desktop-level clicks/keyboard actions. agent_browser is for web automation. rust_native is mainly for advanced debugging and compatibility.
config.form.browser_native_headless_help: Turn on to show a visible browser window for manual login. Turn off to run headless.
permissions.title: Permissions & Security
permissions.description: Manage global browser access and core safety guardrails without exposing low-level shell rules to regular users.
permissions.guardrails_description: Settings here affect every Agent session. Session-level supervised/full mode only decides which actions still require approval.
permissions.scope_note: Browser access is global. Workspace selection, path scope, and one-off approval results remain session-scoped. Low-level shell rules are no longer exposed here.
permissions.browser_description: Control whether the Agent can register and use browser tools for browsing, automation, and page inspection.
permissions.browser_enabled_help: When turned off, the runtime no longer registers the browser / browser_open tools, so the Agent cannot use browser capabilities.
permissions.browser_runtime_note: After saving, the next Agent run rebuilds its runtime tool list from this toggle.
permissions.tool_group_empty: No tools are available in this group.
permissions.tool_approval_title: Built-in Tool Approval In Supervised Mode
permissions.tool_approval_empty: No configurable built-in tools are currently available.
permissions.shell_approval_description: List the commands that should still ask for approval in supervised mode. This replaces the old global allow/block shell rules.
permissions.shell_approval_note: One command per line, such as rm, curl, or ssh. When one of these commands is used, the Agent asks first in supervised mode. Choosing “Always allow” still only applies to the exact command in the current session.
config.channels.title: Communication Channels
config.channels.description: Add or edit external message entry points. The in-app chat still works when no channel is configured.
config.channels.empty_desc: You can keep using the in-app chat now and add Telegram, Slack, email, or another entry point later.
channels.page_hint: All communication channels are shown as cards with clear configuration status. Click a card to review and update its settings.
channels.invalid_json_hint: The current JSON cannot be parsed. Fix it before saving.
channels.modal_update_hint: Changes are saved directly into the current system configuration.
channels.modal_create_hint: Saving enables this communication channel and writes it into config.

doctor.running_hint: This may take a few seconds.
doctor.empty_hint: Click "Run Diagnostics" to check your DeerClaw installation.
auth.enter_code: Enter your pairing code to connect to the agent.

onboard.welcome.subtitle: Let's get your AI assistant set up and ready to go.
onboard.title: DeerClaw Setup
onboard.subtitle: Configure your AI Agent
onboard.provider_config.subtitle: Configure provider API key and endpoint
onboard.tier.recommended.desc: Best balance of performance and ease of use
onboard.tier.local.desc: Run models locally without API keys
onboard.tier.subtitle: Select the operational mode that best fits your needs
onboard.provider.model_help: Leave blank to use the provider's default model.
onboard.identity.title: Identity & Persona
onboard.identity.desc: Configure your agent identity and persona
onboard.identity.comm_style.desc: Used for scheduling and time-aware tasks.
onboard.identity.style.direct: Direct & concise — skip pleasantries, get to the point
onboard.identity.style.friendly: Friendly & casual — warm, human, and helpful
onboard.identity.style.professional: Professional & polished — calm, confident, and clear
onboard.identity.style.playful: Expressive & playful — more personality + natural emojis
onboard.identity.style.custom: Custom — write your own style guide
onboard.identity.style.custom_placeholde: Describe how the agent should speak...
onboard.memory.title: Memory Configuration
onboard.memory.auto_save.desc: Automatically save important conversation details to long-term memory
onboard.features.title: System & Security
onboard.features.sovereign: Do not connect third-party apps yet
onboard.features.sovereign.desc: Start with local and built-in tools. Your third-party accounts are not connected to an external integration platform, and you can enable this later.
onboard.features.composio.desc: Use Composio to connect Gmail, Notion, GitHub, Slack, and more. Requires a Composio API key.
onboard.features.composio.note: DeerClaw still runs locally; third-party authorization and some app actions are handled through Composio and remain constrained by your autonomy and approval settings.
onboard.features.supervised.desc: Agent asks for permission before critical actions.
onboard.features.full.desc: Agent runs freely. For experienced users.
onboard.features.encrypt.desc: Encrypt sensitive config values at rest
onboard.hardware.title: Hardware Control
onboard.hardware.enable.desc: Allow agent to interact with physical devices via serial/probe
onboard.hardware.datasheets.desc: Allow agent to search and read local datasheet PDFs
onboard.channels.title: Communication Channels
onboard.channels.subtitle: Configure communication channels
onboard.channels.more: This onboarding supports quick setup for Telegram and Discord; additional channels can be enabled in config.
onboard.channels.more_all: Select one or more channels and configure each via JSON.
onboard.channels.none_selected: No channel selected. CLI channel remains enabled.
onboard.error.api_unreachable: Cannot connect to local onboarding API. Please ensure ZeroClaw local service is running.
onboard.config.title: Configure {{provider}}
onboard.config.endpoint.help: Full URL to the API endpoint.
onboard.config.model.help: Enter the model name you want to use.
